what_it_does: Implements the ReviewWorkspace React component: a candidate approval/rejection UI that lists pending artifact candidates, focuses one (preferring Vi's variant pick), shows a shared CandidateCard, compares against the most recent approved or rejected same-kind artifact, runs an Auto-QA pass based on binding sources, displays a contextual Vi recommendation, and renders a side-by-side candidate grid.
observations: Uses global window APIs (MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_RESOLVE, MS_VI_BRAIN, MS_KINDS) and optional primitives (CandidateCard, KindSpecimen, Panel, ViNote, Chip, Btn, Icon, Eyebrow, QaRow).
Candidate strip highlights the focused item and marks Vi's pick with a star icon.
Focus is kept valid via useEffect when the candidate pool shrinks after decisions.
Compare mode toggles between an approved anchor and a rejected anti-pattern for the same kind.
Auto-QA maps each binding source to pass/warn, warning when a value fell to a default.
Lineage panel shows origin, resolved value count, QA pass count, variant axis value, and refine status.
A 'What approving teaches' panel visualizes the kind's variant axis and the current candidate's position on it.
Empty state offers navigation to generation and displays recently approved artifacts.
recommendText builds dynamic JSX guidance from pick status, variant, and anchor existence.
standouts: The workspace is built around a real artifact pool and a single decision path rather than mock images.
Vi-pick logic uses MS_RESOLVE.variantBias to select the candidate matching the currently leaned variant.
Auto-QA is grounded on binding provenance (explicit/preference/memory/ai vs default) instead of static heuristics.
conventions: React functional component with hooks, aliased as useStateRev/useEffectRev/useMemoRev.
Global window namespace used as the module system; components check window.* before rendering.
BEM-like CSS class names and inline style objects mixed in JSX.
Conditional rendering with short-circuit and ternary expressions.
concerns: window.useArtifacts() and window.useFeedbackStore() are called directly inside the render body on every render, which may trigger side effects outside React's hook rules.
Many global APIs are accessed without guards in some render paths (e.g., window.MS_RESOLVE.axisFor inside the Lineage panel).
Inline gridTemplateColumns style is hardcoded to '170px 1fr 320px'.
window.ViContext.set is invoked inside an effect without cleanup or error handling.
recommendText reaches for window.MS_RESOLVE directly, assuming it exists.
notes: A real-data candidate-review dashboard that wires global artifact, resolution, and Vi-brain APIs into a focused approve/reject UI.
questions: What exact contract does MS_PRODUCE.decide expose, since it is mentioned in comments but not called here?
Are window.useArtifacts and window.useFeedbackStore React hooks or plain subscription functions?
What is the guaranteed shape of an artifact object (bindings, contentPrev, origin, values, variant)?
How does CandidateCard handle approve/reject actions and the refine flow?
What is the precise return shape of MS_RESOLVE.axisFor and the semantics of variantBias?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the described migration from mock review to real artifact review with all listed features.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for candidates
intention_signals: To turn the review workspace into a live, decision-teaching interface where approving or rejecting real generated artifacts updates the system's memory and biases future generation toward preferred variants.
novelty: The integration of a 'Vi pick' based on a resolution bias function, and an Auto-QA rule set derived from binding source provenance rather than static heuristics.
contribution: A concrete React workspace that surfaces pending artifacts, anchors decisions against prior approved or rejected same-kind artifacts, exposes provenance-based QA, and renders Vi guidance and variant-leaning feedback.
summary_for_embedding: ReviewWorkspace is a React component that renders a real-artifact candidate review UI with a candidate strip, focused card, anchor/anti-pattern comparison, provenance-based Auto-QA, Vi recommendation, and side-by-side grid.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the approve/reject decision surface that updates the shared artifact pool and teaches the generation system via variant bias and memory.

symbols.does: Selects the most recent approved or rejected artifact of the same kind as the focused candidate.
symbols.description: A small helper closure inside ReviewWorkspace that filters the full artifact list by the focused candidate's kind_id and the requested status, then returns the last matching artifact (or undefined). It powers the anchor and anti-pattern comparisons.

symbols.does: Builds a contextual Vi recommendation string for the focused artifact.
symbols.description: A pure function that constructs JSX text explaining whether the focused artifact is Vi's current pick, what variant axis value it carries, and whether an approved anchor exists; it personalizes the recommendation rather than returning static boilerplate.
